name: Shree Kshetra Arihantagiri
description: Traces back over 2,300 years to the 2nd Century BCE. Believed to be where the last Shrutkevali Bhadrabahu resided with 8,000 saints for meditation.
introText: Significant Digambar Jain pilgrimage center featuring four temples and a prominent 18-foot high idol of Tirthankar Neminath (Shikhamani Nadar). The complex includes ancient monk caves with rare paintings and carvings.

name: Arihanthgiri Ancient Mountain Temples
description: Famous for the Samadhi of Muni Sri Vadeebha Simha and 12th-century Chola-period artistic influences.
introText: Mountain top segment of the Arihantagiri complex featuring the 18-foot Neminath idol and 12th-century rock-cut shrines.

name: Arani Saidapet Jain Temple
introText: Ancient temple in Saidapet, Arani, dedicated to Tirthankar Ananthanath.

name: Shri 1008 Bhagwan Adinath Digambar Jain Temple, Arani
introText: Significant spiritual center located in Kosapalayam, Arani, dedicated to Lord Adinath.

name: Poondi Ponezhil Nathar Digamber Jain (Arugar) Temple
description: Built in 1305 CE by King Veerasamban during the Chola era. Protected by ASI.
introText: 700-year-old temple also known as Veeraveera Jinalayam, featuring a 3-foot Lord Parshwanath idol. Notable for Chola architecture.

name: Ancient Jain Temple Munigiri, Karandai
description: Hosted major religious debates. Received patronage from Chola and Vijayanagar dynasties.
introText: 8th-century heritage center associated with Acharya Akalanka. Features Kunthunath, Parshwanathar, and Adinathar temples.

name: Thiruppanamoor Digambar Jain Temple, Karandai
description: Site where munis attained sallekhana. Features a unique two-tiered sanctum.
introText: 15th-century temple dedicated to Lord Pushpadanta featuring rare gilded plaster images and footprints of ancient munis.

name: Thiruparuthikundram Sri Digambar Jain Temples, Kanchivaram
description: Built by Pallava King Narasimhavarman II. Features a 2000-year-old Kura tree. Protected by Tamil Nadu Archaeology Dept.
introText: Known as Jain Kanchi, this 8th-century complex features the Trilokyanatha Temple with 14th-century Vijayanagar roof paintings.

name: Acharya Kund Kund Charan, Ponnur Hill
description: One of the most significant sites for Digambar Jains due to its association with Kundkund Acharya.
introText: Sacred hill where Acharya Kundkund (author of Samayasara) practiced penance. Features his footprints at the peak.

name: Sri Adinath Digamber Jain Temple, Ponnur Malai
introText: Base temple featuring a 30-inch marble Adinath idol and a recently installed 24-foot Bahubali statue.

name: Thirakoil Jain Hill
description: Protected ASI monument. Features rare 8th-century rock-cut architecture.
introText: 8th-century site with cave abodes for monks and a 25-foot monolithic stone with carvings of four Tirthankaras.

name: Ariyampoondi Rock Cut Mahavir Jain Temple
introText: Rock-cut shrine in Poondi village near Arani. Often associated with the 700-year-old Ponnezhilnathar complex.

name: Sri 1008 Adinath Digambar Jain Temple, Ponnur
introText: Historic Swarnapuram site featuring an 18th-century inscription tablet and heavy spiritual presence.

name: Ancient Bhagwan Adinath Temple, Salukkai
description: Dates back to Chola era (11th century).
introText: 11th-century site known as Veera Kerala Perum Palli. Features a 5-foot granite statue of Lord Adinath.

name: 1008 Bhagavan Sri Adinath Digambar Jain Basadi, Vizhukkam
introText: 16th-century Dravidian style temple featuring fortified walls and beautiful outer wall carvings of Tirthankaras.

name: Mel Chitamur (Jin Kanchi)
introText: Primary religious center (Math) for Tamil Jains. Features a 14-foot black Parsvanatha idol and 52-foot manastambha.

name: Thirunathan Kundru Jain Hill, Gingee
introText: Rich heritage site near Gingee with rock carvings of all 24 Tirthankaras and 5th-century inscriptions.

name: Shri Aadinath Digambar Jain Temple, Srirangapatna
introText: 1660 AD temple housing idols of Goddess Padmavathi and the 24 Tirthankaras.

name: Shri Parshwanath Digambar Jain Temple and Jain Boarding Dharmashala, Mysuru
introText: Centrally located temple in Mysuru on Chandragupta Road.

name: Gommatgiri
introText: Famous for its 700-year-old 16-foot high monolithic statue of Bahubali atop Shravana Gudda.

name: Shri Baahubali Theertha, Kaaveri Dhaama, (Basadi Hosakote)
description: Statue consecrated during the reign of Hoysala king Vishnuvardhana. Recently held a major Mastakabhisheka with Cauvery river water.
introText: Significant heritage center featuring an 18-foot-tall monolithic statue of Lord Bahubali established in 1147 CE. Located near the KRS Dam with scenic river views.

name: Chandragiri Hill, Sravanbelgola
description: Final resting place of Emperor Chandragupta Maurya who took sallekhana here in 298 BCE alongside Bhadrabahu Svami.
introText: One of the two sacred hills in Shravanabelagola, housing various Basadis and ancient inscriptions.

name: Vindhyagiri Hill, Sravanabelgola
description: The statue was built in 981-983 AD by Chavundaraya, the minister of the Ganga dynasty. Host of the grand Mahamastakabhisheka festival every 12 years.
introText: World-famous hill featuring the 57-foot monolithic statue of Lord Gommateshwara (Bahubali), carved from a single granite block.

name: Jain Mutt Temple, Sravanabelgola
introText: The administrative and religious head of the Shravanabelagola complex, featuring beautiful wall paintings (murals) depicted in ancient styles.

name: Jinnathpuram
introText: Small village near Shravanabelagola known for the Aregal Basadi and historical connections to the Ganga dynasty.

name: Kambadahalli Jain Mutt
description: One of the oldest Jain centers in Karnataka. The Panchakuta Basadi is an ASI protected monument showing 10th-century craftsmanship.
introText: Ancient center featuring the famous Panchakuta Basadi, a masterpiece of Western Ganga architecture.

name: Shri Paras Dham Atishay Kshetra
introText: Spiritual center focused on the teachings of Rashtrasant Param Gurudev Shree Namramuni Maharaj Saheb, dedicated to Lord Parshwanath.

name: Mandargiri Hill, Tumkur
introText: Also known as Basadi Betta, famous for its unique Peacock-feather shaped (Pinchi) Guru Mandir and 81-foot monolithic statue of Lord Chandranatha.

name: Shri Digambar Jain Temple, Mangaluru
introText: Ancient temple in the Bunder area of Mangaluru, primarily dedicated to Lord Shanthinath.

name: Saavira Kambada Basadi, Moodbadri
description: Built in 1430 AD. A pinnacle of Vijayanagara architecture in coastal Karnataka.
introText: Known as the "Thousand Pillar Temple," it is the most magnificent of Moodbidri's 18 Basadis, featuring no two pillars with the same carving.

name: Vennoor Bahubali
description: Installed in 1604 AD by Timmanna Ajila, the ruler of the Ajila dynasty.
introText: Features a 35-foot monolithic Bahubali statue erected on an elevated platform on the banks of the Phalguni river.

name: Adinath Swamy Basadi, Vennoor
introText: Significant 17th-century Basadi in Venur associated with the local ruling dynasties.

name: Dharmasthala Bahubali
description: Sculpted by Renjala Gopala Shenoy in 1973. Symbolizes the "Sarva-Dharma" harmony of Dharmasthala.
introText: A 39-foot monolithic statue situated on Ratnagiri hill, heart of the Dharmasthala pilgrimage center.

name: Ratnatraya Basadi Atishay Kshetra
introText: Tri-shrine complex (Trikoota) in Bilagi, dedicated to the three jewels of Jainism.

name: Karkala Bahubali
description: Consecrated in 1432 AD by Veera Pandya, King of the Santara dynasty.
introText: Features a 42-foot monolithic Bahubali statue, the second tallest in Karnataka.

name: Chaturmukh Basadi, Karkala
description: ASI protected monument. Commissioned by Immadi Bhairarasa Vodeya in the late 16th century.
introText: Unique four-faced temple built entirely of granite, featuring four identical entrances facing the four cardinal directions.

name: Varanga Lake Jain Temple
introText: Known as Kere Basadi, this beautiful 12th-century star-shaped temple is located in the middle of a scenic lake, accessible only by boat.

name: Kundadri Shri Parshwanath Basadi
description: Associated with the 4th-century monk Acharya Kundakunda who performed penance here.
introText: Ancient temple situated atop the Kundadri hill, surrounded by dense evergreen forests and rock ponds.

name: Jwalamalini Atishay Kshetra, N R Pura
introText: Powerful center dedicated to Goddess Jwalamalini, the Yakshi of Lord Chandraprabha. Famous for its annual Rathotsava.

name: Humcha Jain Temple
description: Established in the 8th century CE as the capital of the Santara dynasty. One of India's most important Padmavati shrines.
introText: Extremely sacred site dedicated to Goddess Padmavati and Lord Parshwanatha, known for the "Panchakuta Basadi" and a lake that reputedly never dries.

name: Ramtek Jain Temples
description: Believed to be built by Ramchandraji as per Padmapuran. Associated with the poet Kalidas who composed 'Meghdoot' nearby.
introText: Significant ancient Tirth featuring a 4-meter golden-colored idol of Lord Shantinatha. The complex includes nine temples and a beautiful Manastambh.

name: Seoni Bada Mandir
introText: Prominent Digambar Jain temple in Seoni city, known for its spiritual atmosphere and cultural significance.

name: Chhapara Jain Mandir
introText: Historic Jain temple located in Chhapara, serving the local Digambar community.

name: Lakhnadaun Jain Mandir (Samnapur Jinalay)
introText: Sacred site in Lakhnadaun (Samnapur) dedicated to the Digambar tradition.

name: Pisanhari ki Madhiya Ji, Jabalpur
description: Named after the founder who ground flour to save for the construction. Her original quern stones are preserved.
introText: 500-year-old temple complex on a 300-ft hill, built by a poor woman (Pisanhari) from her savings. Features 13 temples and a 55-foot Bahubali statue.

name: Shasanoday Tirth, Hanumantal, Jabalpur
introText: Historic center in the Hanumantal area of Jabalpur, serving as a key Digambar pilgrimage site.

name: Koni Ji Atishay Kshetra, Paatan
introText: Ancient miraculous holy place (Atishay Kshetra) in Paatan, known for its historic idols.

name: Beena Baarah Atishay Kshetra
description: Named after King Benu. The 16-foot Mahavir idol is believed to be one-of-a-kind.
introText: Miraculous site near the Sukh-Chain river featuring a unique 16-foot limestone idol of Lord Mahavir in sitting posture.

name: Patnaganj Jinalay, Rehli
introText: Significant Digambar Jinalay located in the Rehli/Patnaganj region of Sagar district.

name: Pateriya Ji Atishay Kshetra, Garhakota
description: Built in 1782 AD with a single day's cotton trade earnings. Known for the "Atishaya Kund" where pond water turned to ghee.
introText: 200-year-old miraculous site featuring three 7-feet high black stone idols of Lord Parshvanath.

name: Shri Bade Baba, Kundalpur
description: Ancient 8th-century site rediscovered with help from Bundela ruler Chhatrasal. Place where Sri Sridhar Kevali attained Moksha.
introText: One of India's most important Digambar sites, featuring the 15-foot "Bade Baba" (Lord Adinath) idol. A massive new 189-foot Nagara-style temple is under construction.

name: Kundalpur Bade Baba Parvat
introText: The sacred mountain at Kundalpur housing 46 temples, including the primary Bade Baba shrine.

name: Chetanoday Tirth, Katni
introText: Modern Tirth in Katni focused on spiritual awakening and education.

name: Shri Parshwanath Jinalay, Bilhari
introText: Ancient site in Bilhari known for its historic carvings and Tirthankar idols.

name: Bahoriband Atishay Kshetra
introText: Miraculous site in Katni district with ancient monolithic statues and inscriptions.

name: Sammed Giri Tirth, Gosalpur
introText: Sacred hill tirth in Gosalpur providing a serene environment for meditation.

name: Panagar Atishay Kshetra
description: Rich in Kalchuri era art. The 9-foot idol is a masterpiece of medieval sculpture.
introText: 10th-century site featuring a 9-foot high idol of Lord Shantinath discovered via excavation from a pond.

name: Chandragiri Hill, Sravanabelgola
description: Final resting place of Emperor Chandragupta Maurya who took sallekhana here in 298 BCE alongside Bhadrabahu Svami.
introText: One of the two sacred hills in Shravanabelagola, housing various Basadis and ancient inscriptions.

name: Jain Mutt Temple
introText: The administrative head of the Shravanabelagola shrine. Known for its historical library and significant role in Jain history.

name: Aghapura Jain Temple, Hyderabad
introText: Significant 1008 Chandraprabhu Digambar temple in Hyderabad, known for its architectural grandeur and spiritual energy.

name: Shri Kalikund Parshwanath, Hathnoora
description: Cultural and spiritual hub for the local Digambar community in Sangareddy district.
introText: Significant Atishay Kshetra (place of miracles) located in Hathnoora, Telangana, dedicated to Lord Parshwanath.

name: Jogipet Jain Temple
introText: Local Jain temple in Jogipet serving the historical Jain population of Telangana.

name: Shri Vighnahar Parshwanath, Kulcharam
introText: Known for its architectural beauty and the 'Vighnahar' (remover of obstacles) idol of Lord Parshwanath.

name: Penukonda Jain Temples
description: Established as a Jain Mutt seat in 1359 AD. One of the four traditional Bhattaraka centers of learning in the south.
introText: Ancient 14th-century site featuring the Sri Parshwanath and Sri Ajithanath temples. Known for the unique 'Pache' (green) Parshwanath idol.

name: Konda Kunda, Konakondla
description: One of the most significant sites for Digambar Jains due to its foundational connection to Kundakunda's lineage.
introText: Historic village known as the birthplace of the legendary 4th-century monk Acharya Kundakunda.

name: Belagavi Kamal Basadi
description: Built in 1204 AD by the Ratta dynasty. An ASI protected monument dedicated to Lord Neminatha.
introText: 1200-year-old Chalukya-style temple located inside the Belagavi Fort. Famous for its 72-petal monolithic lotus carving on the ceiling.

name: Sankeshwar Shri Parshwanath Jain Temple
introText: Significant pilgrimage site on the Bangalore-Mysore highway dedicated to Lord Parshwanath.

name: Shri Kshetra Nischay Giri
introText: Spiritual hill near Kothali known for its serene environment and association with modern Jain ascetics.

name: Atishay Kshetra Stavnidhi
introText: Ancient miraculous site featuring multiple temples and a historic idol of Lord Navagraha Parshwanath.

name: Shree Kshetra Shanti Giri Kothali
introText: Prominent Digambar center and education hub associated with the lineage of Acharya Shantisagar Ji.

name: Acharya Shri Shanti Sagar Janm Bhoomi, Bhoj
description: Highly revered as the starting point of the modern Digambar revival.
introText: Birthplace of the first Digambar Acharya of the 20th century, Shantisagar Ji Maharaj.

name: Acharya Shri Vidhya Sagar Janm Bhoomi, Sadalga
introText: Birthplace of the highly revered modern saint Acharya Vidhyasagar Ji Maharaj.

name: Kagwad Leni Temple
introText: Underground architectural spiritual marvel featuring multiple basements with Tirthankara idols.

name: Shedbal Ashram
introText: Historic ashram and temple complex in Shedbal, acting as a key spiritual retreat.

name: Ugar Padmavati Atishya Kshetra
introText: Miraculous site in Ugarbudruk, Belagavi district, dedicated to Goddess Padmavati.

name: Hubballi Gabbur Shwetambar Jain Temple
introText: Beautiful Shwetambar temple in Gabbur, Hubli, known for its intricate marble work.

name: Navagraha Teerth Kshetra, Varur
introText: Modern pilgrimage site featuring India's tallest (61-foot) monolithic statue of Lord Parshwanatha, representing the nine planets.

name: Adinath Shantinath Jain Temple, Tadas
introText: Ancient double-shrine (Dwikuta) temple in Tadas, featuring beautiful Chalukyan influences.

name: Acharya Shri Punyasagar Maharaj Janm Bhoomi, Artal
introText: Commemorative temple at the birthplace of Acharya Punyasagar Ji, located in Artal, Karnataka.

name: Acharya Pujyapad Swami Upsarg Sthali, Banavasi
Shri Atishay Kshetra Vadanbailu Padmavati
introText: Miraculous Padmavati shrine located in a serene forest setting near Jog Falls.

name: Chaturmukha Basadi, Gerusoppa
description: Former capital of the Saluva dynasty. Shows fusion of Chalukya and Vijayanagara styles.
introText: 14th-century architectural masterpiece with four identical entrances, built by the 'Pepper Queen' Chennabhairadevi.

name: Muni Shri Payasagar Maharaj Janm Bhoomi, Halsasi
introText: Birthplace temple of Muni Payasagar Maharaj in the historic village of Halsi, Belagavi.

name: Muni Shri Viditsagar Maharaj Janm Bhoomi, Harige
introText: Commemorative temple at the birthplace of Muni Viditsagar Ji in Harige, Shimoga.

name: NR Pura Atishay Kshetra
introText: Jwalamalini Atishay Kshetra in Narasimharajapura, a powerful shrine for Goddess Jwalamalini.

name: Madhuban (Shikharji Base)
introText: The base camp and primary religious junction for climbing Sammed Shikharji. Hosts various Dharmshalas and Kothis.

name: Sammed Shikharji Parvat Vandana
description: Most sacred site for all Jain sects. Includes Jal-Mandir and various Tonks (shrines) dedicated to Tirthankaras.
introText: The holiest peaks in Jainism where 20 of the 24 Tirthankaras attained Nirvana.

name: Palganj Prachin Digambar Jain Mandir
introText: Ancient temple in Palganj, traditionally visited by pilgrims on the way to Sammed Shikharji.

name: Mandargiri Hill, Nirvan Bhoomi of Vasupujya Swami
introText: Sacred hill near Champapuri marking the Nirvana site of Lord Vasupujya Swami, the 12th Tirthankara.

name: Mandargiri Base Temple
introText: The base temple serving pilgrims visiting Mandargiri Hill in Bihar.

name: Champapuri, Bhagalpur
introText: Unique Siddhakshtera where all five Kalyanakas of Tirthankar Vasupujya occurred. 2500-year-old ancient temple.

name: Kundalpur (Nalanda) Digambar Jain Mandir
introText: Revered as the birthplace of Lord Mahavira (24th Tirthankar) and Gautam Swami. Features a 4.5-foot Mahavira idol.

name: Pavapuri Jal Mandir
description: Site where Mahavira attained salvation in 490 BC. The lake was formed by devotees collecting sacred earth from the cremation site.
introText: Magnificent white marble temple in the middle of a lotus pond, marking the Nirvana place of Lord Mahavira.

name: Gunavaji Siddha Kshetra, Nawada
introText: Nirvana place of Indrabhuti Gautam Ganadhar (chief disciple of Mahavira). Temple is in the middle of a pond.

name: Digambar Jain Dharmshala, Rajgir
introText: Primary accommodation facility for Digambar pilgrims in the holy city of Rajgir.

name: Bhagwaan Munisuvratnath Swami Janm Bhoomi, Rajgir
description: Celebrates the Garbha, Janma, Tapa, and Gyan Kalyanakas of the Lord.
introText: Birthplace of the 20th Tirthankar Munisuvratnath. Known as 'Lal Mandir'.

name: Parvat Vandana, Rajgir
introText: Pilgrimage to the five hills of Rajgir (Vipulachala, Ratnagiri, etc.) where many saints attained liberation.
